authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.21 - Condition of participation: Quality assessment and performance improvement program
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital QAPI regulation. It directly supports hospital-wide quality assessment, performance improvement, adverse event, medical error, and corrective-action expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality control baseline and QAPI evidence validation
used_for: hold, release, nonconformance, and corrective-action boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to evidence-backed quality operations and formal escalation rather than final QAPI authority.

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.24 - Condition of participation: Medical record services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital medical-record regulation. It directly supports EHR or medical-record truth set, documentation, authentication, record completeness, and audit traceability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: medical record and evidence traceability validation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires quality findings, holds, release recommendations, and CAPA packages to cite source records instead of narrative assumptions.

authoritative_sources.title: State Operations Manual Appendix A - Survey Protocol, Regulations and Interpretive Guidelines for Hospitals
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS hospital survey and interpretive guidance for Conditions of Participation. It supports survey, QAPI, patient safety, medical record, and compliance evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: regulatory boundary and policy conflict handling
used_for: quality and safety survey evidence posture
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the spec from inventing hospital quality thresholds when CMS interpretive guidance or tenant policy must be checked.

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule and Care Coordination
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. Its guidance is authoritative for PHI use and disclosure in treatment, payment, and health care operations contexts, including quality operations.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains patient-level quality outputs and requires privacy escalation when use, disclosure, or recipient authority is unclear.

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR minimum-necessary guidance is authoritative for limiting operational PHI disclosures, role-based distribution, and privacy escalation conditions.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces the lane to hold PHI distribution until purpose, recipients, access, and minimum-necessary scope are documented.

authoritative_sources.title: Hospital Inpatient Quality Reporting Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS hospital quality reporting program source. It grounds hospital quality measure submission, reporting period, program status, and public reporting terminology.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports measure-specific evidence expectations while keeping final reporting attestation outside the lane.

authoritative_sources.title: Inpatient Hospital Specifications Manuals
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services / QualityNet
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: QualityNet is the CMS-approved quality data exchange and specifications channel for hospital quality reporting. It grounds measure abstraction, eCQM, numerator, denominator, exclusion, and submission evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: measure abstraction and submission evidence validation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains in-scope status summaries to documented measure fields and source references rather than informal quality labels.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Measures Management System Blueprint - Measure Lifecycle Overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS measure lifecycle guidance is authoritative for measure development, testing, implementation, maintenance, and evidence terminology used by CMS quality programs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: measure terminology and evidence lifecycle normalization
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supplies vendor-neutral measure language for quality operations without granting final reporting or measure interpretation authority.

authoritative_sources.title: Communication and Optimal Resolution (CANDOR) Toolkit
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is a federal healthcare quality and patient-safety agency. CANDOR is a public patient-safety event response and resolution workflow reference.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: patient-safety event response and escalation packaging
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports incident package structure while preserving formal patient-safety, risk, legal, and clinical review authority.

authoritative_sources.title: AHRQ Common Formats for Event Reporting
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ Common Formats provide public patient-safety event reporting taxonomy and data elements for event, near miss, unsafe condition, and harm reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: event taxonomy and patient-safety evidence handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Gives the lane precise event vocabulary but requires escalation before harm severity or event classification is finalized.

authoritative_sources.title: Serious Reportable Events
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NQF's Serious Reportable Events are a recognized public patient-safety taxonomy for serious, largely preventable adverse events used in hospital quality and safety operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: serious event terminology and escalation trigger handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from downgrading or closing serious event-like issues without formal authority.

authoritative_sources.title: Sentinel Event Policy and Procedures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Joint Commission is a recognized hospital accrediting body. Its public sentinel event policy supports event triage, review, corrective-action, and formal authority boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: sentinel event and formal corrective-action escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Ensures sentinel event, RCA, and CAPA closure requests are routed to formal authorities instead of handled by the specification-based lane.

authoritative_sources.title: National Healthcare Safety Network Patient Safety Component Manual
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC NHSN is the national public health surveillance system for healthcare-associated infection and patient-safety reporting modules used by hospitals.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: patient-safety surveillance and infection-related quality evidence handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports infection or surveillance-adjacent quality evidence handling while routing interpretation to infection prevention or formal reporting owners.

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Required prerequisites include tenant facility, jurisdiction, care setting, program, quality criterion or measure, review workflow, approval matrix, QAPI charter, RACI, source hierarchy, escalation SLA, patient/member status, service date, payer or plan, provider type, authorization status, referral dependency, eligibility and benefits status, privacy and consent constraints, minimum-necessary rule, EHR or EMR evidence, incident or QAPI tracker state, measure abstraction record, audit trail, and any coding, claim, denial, appeal, or payer-portal record that materially affects the quality disposition. Missing authorization, referral, eligibility, source-record, reviewer, privacy, or approval evidence blocks release or closure.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems include the EHR or EMR and clinical documentation system, incident reporting system, QAPI or quality management tracker, patient-safety log, quality measure abstraction and submission tools, case management and utilization review systems, patient access or CRM systems, eligibility and benefits systems, approved payer portal evidence, practice management or revenue cycle platform, charge capture system, coding system, claims platform, remittance and denial/appeal system, privacy and access-control system, document repository, and audit logs. Claim, code, coverage, medical-necessity, payer-policy, and clinical truth remain with the accountable adjacent owners rather than this lane.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, triage, clinical significance, harm severity, sentinel or serious event determination, root cause approval, QAPI committee action, peer review conclusion, infection-prevention determination, discharge readiness, level-of-care or medical necessity decision, utilization approval or denial, code assignment, claim adjudication, reimbursement interpretation, authorization approval, appeal strategy, PHI disclosure approval, legal interpretation, financial approval, or safety-critical authority. Those require adjacent owner escalation or orchestrator return.

workflow_stages.name: Quality intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture facility, business unit, care setting, requested disposition, quality criterion, measure, incident type, patient/member context, source systems, payer/plan, privacy rule, review workflow, approval matrix, and authority context before accepting work.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and source-record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate EHR or EMR records, incident reports, QAPI trackers, patient-safety logs, measure abstraction records, audit logs, eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, payer portal, case management, utilization review, coding, claim, denial, appeal, and owner records that materially affect quality disposition.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Control, inspection, and measure evidence assessment
workflow_stages.description: Apply the tenant quality criterion, QAPI expectation, measure specification, or inspection checklist to source evidence and classify the status as satisfied for authorized review, blocked, held, nonconforming, investigation-needed, corrective-action-needed, or escalated.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Defect, incident, and nonconformance handling
workflow_stages.description: Package incidents, nonconformances, recurring defects, missing evidence, conflicting records, patient-safety signals, privacy-sensitive requests, and clinical or payer boundary cases for the accountable owner without making formal clinical, safety, privacy, payer, or approval decisions.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Hold, release, CAPA, and review routing
workflow_stages.description: Maintain hold or unresolved-risk status until source evidence, reviewer support, owner acceptance, verification evidence, PHI posture, and audit trail are complete; prepare release recommendation, CAPA escalation, or unresolved-risk routing package for authorized review.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: case management or utilization review record
privacy approval or minimum-necessary routing note
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation systems
quality measure abstraction and submission system
patient access, CRM, eligibility, and benefits systems
practice management and revenue cycle platform
claims, remittance, denial, and appeal systems

allowed: Classify a hospital quality operations request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request or validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed quality status, hold, release recommendation, nonconformance, findings, incident, CAPA, and unresolved-risk summaries.

Determine whether documented quality or safety operations evidence supports continued hold, release recommendation for authorized review, investigation, nonconformance, corrective-action escalation, or orchestrator return.
Route work to the correct adjacent specialist, approver, committee, or human reviewer when ownership shifts.
escalate: Escalate when PHI handling, privacy obligations, consent, access scope, or minimum-necessary distribution changes the answer.
Escalate when patient-safety, clinical risk, harm status, serious event, sentinel event, infection prevention, peer review, RCA, or formal QAPI authority is implicated.
Escalate when authorization, referral, eligibility, medical necessity, payer policy, coding, claim, denial, appeal, or reimbursement interpretation is required.
Escalate when public, tenant, payer, accreditation, state, contract, or facility policy conflicts materially.
Escalate when source systems conflict or the accountable reviewer, approver, or adjacent owner cannot be identified.
refuse: Refuse diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical significance, harm severity, discharge readiness, level of care, medical necessity, or licensed review decisions.
Refuse PHI disclosure approval, patient-level extract distribution, or privacy-rule interpretation without authorized privacy review.
Refuse payer-policy interpretation, authorization approval, coverage decision, code assignment, claim correction, reimbursement decision, write-off, or appeal strategy.
Refuse to bypass hold, release, safety-control, nonconformance, CAPA, QAPI, or final review boundaries without explicit authority.
Refuse to close quality work when required source evidence, review support, owner acceptance, verification evidence, or audit trail is missing or contradictory.

domain_failure_modes: Patient or member harm from bad routing, missing escalation, premature release, unsupported closure, or incorrect assumption about clinical risk.
PHI exposure or privacy violation from broad quality extract distribution, missing minimum-necessary analysis, missing consent constraint, or incomplete access audit.
Compliance exposure from unsupported QAPI, measure, incident, sentinel event, patient-safety, or CAPA handling.
Revenue leakage, claim denial, or audit exposure from unsupported quality handling of authorization, referral, eligibility, coding, claim, denial, remittance, or appeal artifacts.
False quality assurance from treating incomplete, contradictory, stale, or inaccessible source records as sufficient.
Boundary collapse into licensed clinical review, patient-safety authority, payer interpretation, medical coding, revenue cycle, legal, financial, or executive approval.
Loss of traceability when holds, release recommendations, reviewer acceptance, CAPA verification, or closure evidence are not tied to authoritative systems of record.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
Evidence is incomplete for release or signoff
A user tries to close quality work without review support
Request to bypass a documented safety or quality control

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific adverse event reporting thresholds and timelines
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: State reporting rules can materially change incident routing. The validated pack treats state policy as tenant-required runtime context and escalates when it affects disposition.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant peer-review privilege and QAPI committee charter
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Peer-review protections and QAPI approval authority are tenant- and jurisdiction-specific. The spec records them as required tenant facts and refuses final closure without authority evidence.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Accreditation and payer contract precedence over public baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Accreditation standards and payer contracts can narrow local handling. The spec requires conflict escalation rather than guessing precedence.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for the validated specification-based package because jurisdiction, facility, payer, peer-review, accreditation, and tenant policy differences are represented as required runtime facts and refusal or escalation triggers.
The final spec pack is constrained by researched public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, and recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance.

The research output directly drives required scenarios for missing authorization/referral/eligibility, PHI handling, clinical or payer boundary escalation, incomplete evidence, release bypass, incident escalation, repeat defect, closure without review support, conflicting policy, and tenant adaptation.
